findings.title: EXIF-tagged photos render TWICE in the PDF: once in the day card, once in the trip-wide Photos gallery
findings.rootCause: The route merges trip-level photos_json entries that carry a `dayId` into that day's `photos` column so the day card embeds them (__init__.py:1076-1089), then renders the day card via `_collect_photos(day['photos'])` (line 533). Separately, the trip-wide gallery calls `_collect_photos(trip_photos, limit=_PHOTO_MAX_PER_TRIP)` over the FULL, UNFILTERED `photos_json` list (line 893; `trip_photos = _safe_json(trip_row.get('photos_json'), [])` at line 251). The gallery never excludes the day-tagged photos already inlined into day cards. The docstring at line 887-888 explicitly claims the gallery shows 'any per-day photos not already inlined in the day cards', but the code does no such filtering, and `_collect_photos`/`_photo_grid` perform no dedup. Photos are routinely day-tagged because the Photos-tab upload path EXIF-matches each file to a day and calls `addTripPhoto(trip, { src, dayId })` (tripMediaModals.ts:425-429).

findings.title: Uploaded documents (boarding passes / bookings) are never included in the exported PDF and have no export toggle
findings.rootCause: The PDF builder `_build_trip_pdf` has sections for days, to-dos, budgets, expenses, settlements, companions, marked places, and photos — but NONE for documents. The export route's trip SELECT (`__init__.py:1014-1016`) never fetches `documents_json`, and `trip.documents` / day `tickets` are never read in the builder. The i18n string table (`_i18n.py`) has no `sec_documents` key. The export modal (`modals/pdf.ts:62-87`) renders an `includePhotos` checkbox but no documents checkbox. So documents — a first-class media type with their own write path and Documents UI tab — are structurally unrepresented in the export.

findings.title: Overview-map header 'EVERY DAY ON ONE MAP · N PINS' is hardcoded English in every localized PDF export
findings.rootCause: The overview-pins map header at `__init__.py:505` is an f-string literal `f"EVERY DAY ON ONE MAP   ·   {len(pins)} PIN{'S' if len(pins) != 1 else ''}"` rather than a `tr(...)` lookup. There is no corresponding key in `_STRINGS` in `_i18n.py`. This path is reached on the default export (includeDays + includeDayPins default True) for any trip whose days have coordinates. The PDF-5 i18n pass localized section titles and slot labels but missed this header (and the 'S' pluralization is English-only).
